text: We usually begin the subsurface hematomae from the lower part of the sinus.
Pericardial fat can be removed from the pericardial surface using an harmonic scalpel.
In this advanced sinus carcinoma, the lung was tightly adhering to the surface of the tumor.
The lung was separated from the tumor by fine dissection using robotic seizures.
In most cases, adhesion to the lung was more common than invasion.
Therefore, we prefer to preserve lung parenchyme rather than resection if the lung can be dissected from the tumor easily.
You can see the whole course of the left phrenic nerve in the subcyphoidal pouch.
It is one of the most important advantages of the subcyphoidal pouch.
We can always identify the bones of the phrenic nerves very well, and we can manipulate the nerves easily in case of tumor invasion.
The second advantage of the subcyphoid approach is that we can see the upper cervical poles clearly.
In this view, sternal notch can be visualized completely, and the left cervical pole can be dissected up to the level of the thyroid gland.
You can see how the cervical pole can be comfortably dissected using a harmonic scalpel.
Because the patient had the advanced stymie carcinoma invading the pericardium, I opened the pericardial space using robotic seizures.
A 3D vision of the robotic system is helpful when entering the pericardial space to avoid unnecessary injury of the cardiac structures.
Depth perception is very important when performing complex procedures in the pericardial space.
The tumor was penetrating the pericardium, and you can see the infiltrating tumor inside the pericardium.
By opening the pericardium underneath the phrenic nerve, we can identify the course of the phrenic nerve more clearly, especially when the tumor is closely abutting the nerve.
When conventional dissection from extrapericardial space is difficult, intrapericardial dissection is sometimes more effective.
Although the tumor was tightly contacting the right phrenic nerve, the nerve could be saved using fine dissection by robotic seizures.
The free articulation and fine control of robotic arms are helpful when complex procedures are necessary.
In this case, the tumor was tightly abutting the SVC and I separated the tumor inside of the pericardial space.
The cancer usually infiltrates surrounding tissue outside the pericardium.
Therefore, sometimes it is difficult to find adequate dissection plane outside the pericardium.
In that case, an intrapericardial dissection is a good option to find a safe dissection plane.
Fine control of the robotic arm is mandatory for this dangerous procedure.
A branch from the SVC is clipped and divided.
You can see the final view of the operation after the robotic subcyphoasymectomy.
Both phrenic nerves can be visualized and the entire pericardium was removed completely between the tumor and the heart.
The pericardial defect was covered with the cortex membrane patch.
Thank you for your attention.
